description: Implement `.agent/specs/sqlite-workflow-compaction-architecture.md` in the `depot` package. Optimize for a working version quickly: keep commits direct to FDB, introduce the new FDB/S3 manifest shapes, add persistent Gasoline workflows per database branch, ship hot compaction first, then add reclaim, pin awareness, namespace-fork safety, cold storage, and legacy cleanup. Read the spec first. Do not route commits through workflows. Use versioned BARE for persisted or workflow wire payloads. Prefer small, verifiable slices and keep each story focused enough for one Ralph iteration.

userStories.title: Add workflow compaction constants and FDB key helpers
userStories.description: As a depot developer, I need the new workflow compaction keyspace available before wiring readers or workflows.
acceptanceCriteria: Add constants for the initial activity and payload limits from the spec: signal 64 KiB, job descriptor 256 KiB, FDB batch 500 keys or 2 MiB, S3 upload 64 MiB or one object, S3 delete 100 objects, activity target 30 seconds
Add key helpers for `BR/{branch}/CMP/root`, `CMP/cold_shard`, `CMP/retired_cold_object`, `CMP/stage/{job_id}/hot_shard`, and `SQLITE_CMP_DIRTY/{database_branch_id}`
Add key helpers for unified `DB_PIN/{database_branch_id}/{pin_id}` and namespace proof keys `NS_FORK_PIN`, `NS_CHILD`, `NSCAT_BY_DB`, and `NS_PROOF_EPOCH`
Key helper tests verify byte ordering for txid, shard id, versionstamp, and object-key hash components
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add versioned payload types for compaction metadata
userStories.description: As a depot developer, I need strongly typed persisted values for the new manifest, dirty marker, pins, and cold-object retirement state.
acceptanceCriteria: Add vbare-backed payloads for `CompactionRoot`, `ColdShardRef`, `RetiredColdObject`, `SqliteCmpDirty`, and `DbHistoryPin`
`CompactionRoot` contains schema version, manifest generation, hot watermark txid, cold watermark txid, and cold watermark versionstamp
`ColdShardRef` contains object key, object generation id, shard id, txid/versionstamp bounds, size, content hash, and publish generation
`RetiredColdObject` contains object key, object generation id, content hash, retired manifest generation, retired_at_ms, delete_after_ms, and delete_state
`DbHistoryPin` supports `DatabaseFork`, `NamespaceFork`, and `Bookmark` kinds and records exact versionstamp and txid cut points
Round-trip tests cover every new persisted payload through the versioned encoding path
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Teach readers the new published manifest fallback
userStories.description: As a SQLite reader, I need reads to understand the new manager-owned SHARD and cold refs before compaction starts publishing them.
acceptanceCriteria: The read path still checks PIDX and DELTA first for normal latest reads
If a referenced DELTA is missing or reclaimed, the read path falls back to the latest published SHARD at or below the read cap
If no SHARD covers the page, the read path can locate a matching published `ColdShardRef` without requiring cold payload fetch to be implemented yet
Empty or absent `CMP/root` behaves like the current system and does not break existing reads
Tests cover DELTA-first reads, SHARD fallback reads, and absent-manifest compatibility
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add workflow signal and durable state types
userStories.description: As a workflow implementer, I need exact signal and state types for the four branch workflows before adding behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Add DB manager signals: `DeltasAvailable`, `HotJobFinished`, `ColdJobFinished`, `ReclaimJobFinished`, and `DestroyDatabaseBranch`
Add companion signals: `RunHotJob`, `RunColdJob`, `RunReclaimJob`, and `DestroyDatabaseBranch`
Signal payloads include job id, base manifest generation, input fingerprint, status, and bounded output refs where applicable
Add durable manager state for companion workflow ids, active hot/cold/reclaim jobs, retry cursors, planning deadlines, and branch stop state
Add durable companion state with `idle`, `running`, and `stopping` states for one active job per branch
Versioned encoding tests cover every signal and workflow state payload
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Create persistent branch workflow skeletons
userStories.description: As a depot operator, I need one manager, hot compacter, cold compacter, and reclaimer workflow per active database branch.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `db_manager`, `db_hot_compacter`, `db_cold_compacter`, and `db_reclaimer` workflows with stable unique tags containing only `database_branch_id`
The manager spawns the three companion workflows and stores their workflow ids in durable manager state
Companion workflows listen while idle and do not complete while the branch is live
The manager loop waits for signals or the nearest planning deadline without publishing a durable `Tick` signal
No workflow performs compaction, publish, or deletion work in this story
A workflow test proves the manager can start, spawn companions, receive `DeltasAvailable`, and return to idle
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Wire dirty marker admission and DeltasAvailable
userStories.description: As the commit path, I need a coalesced workflow wakeup that does not emit one Gasoline signal per commit.
acceptanceCriteria: After commit, conveyer updates `SQLITE_CMP_DIRTY/{database_branch_id}` only when hot or cold lag crosses the configured threshold
The first writer that turns a branch dirty sends `DeltasAvailable` to the branch manager immediately
Continued commits refresh the dirty marker and send throttled `DeltasAvailable` signals instead of one signal per commit
The manager clears the dirty marker only after refreshing FDB and confirming no actionable hot, cold, or reclaim lag
Dirty marker clearing compare-and-clears the exact observed value with a Serializable read conflict
Tests cover first-writer wake, throttled wake, and stale dirty-marker clear rejection
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Implement manager FDB refresh and hot-job planning
userStories.description: As the DB manager, I need to observe current branch state and plan a bounded hot job for the latest head.
acceptanceCriteria: Manager refresh reads branch lifecycle, `META/head`, `CMP/root`, dirty marker, current pins, and relevant commit-owned hot keys
Manager computes hot lag from FDB state instead of trusting `DeltasAvailable` payloads
Manager schedules at most one active hot job per branch and records job id, base manifest generation, input fingerprint, and selected input range in workflow state
Manager sets future planning deadlines for hot, cold, reclaim, and final-settle checks even when no signal arrives
Planning handles absent `CMP/root` by initializing an in-memory candidate root without publishing it yet
Tests cover no-op refresh, first hot-job planning, and duplicate `DeltasAvailable` not creating duplicate active hot jobs
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Build staged hot SHARD output
userStories.description: As the hot compacter workflow, I need to materialize candidate SHARD output under non-reader-visible staging keys.
acceptanceCriteria: Hot compacter accepts `RunHotJob`, validates branch lifecycle and base manifest generation, and reads the planned input fingerprint from FDB
Hot compacter writes staged SHARD chunks under `CMP/stage/{job_id}/hot_shard/...` only
Retrying the same job id writes the same staging keys or no-ops
Hot compacter reports `HotJobFinished` with job id, base generation, input fingerprint, output refs, and checksums
Hot compacter does not write reader-visible SHARD keys, clear PIDX, or delete DELTAs
Tests cover idempotent staged output and stale-base-generation rejection
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Publish latest-head hot compaction output
userStories.description: As the DB manager, I need to install hot compaction output for the latest head so the workflow path produces useful reader-visible data.
acceptanceCriteria: Manager accepts `HotJobFinished` only when job id, kind, base generation, and fingerprint match the active hot job
Hot install transaction Serializable-reads branch lifecycle, `CMP/root`, `META/head`, relevant commit-owned inputs, staged output checksums, and expected PIDX values
Hot install copies staged SHARD output into reader-visible `BR/{branch}/SHARD/{shard_id}/{as_of_txid}/...` keys
Hot install advances `CMP/root.hot_watermark_txid` and `manifest_generation`
PIDX entries are cleared or rewritten only when the expected txid/value still matches
DELTA deletion remains untouched in this story
An integration test proves a branch can commit deltas, run manager/hot workflows, publish SHARD output, and read through the published SHARD after PIDX is cleared
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add direct DB history pins to hot planning
userStories.description: As a branch with forks or bookmarks, I need hot compaction to preserve exact pinned txids instead of only latest head.
acceptanceCriteria: Add helpers to write, read, and delete unified `DB_PIN` records for database forks and bookmarks
Manager refresh includes live `DB_PIN` records and computes coverage targets as latest head plus exact pinned txids
Hot-job planning includes selected pinned txid cut points when they would otherwise keep DELTAs live
Hot install can publish SHARD output at both latest head and selected pinned txids
Pins do not expire by retention-window age and no pin TTL policy is introduced
Tests cover exact pinned txid materialization and prove pinned txid 50 does not get rounded to latest head 100
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Implement FDB DELTA reclaim after coverage proof
userStories.description: As the reclaimer workflow, I need to delete obsolete hot rows only after the manager proves reads no longer depend on them.
acceptanceCriteria: Manager schedules bounded FDB reclaim jobs only after recomputing manifest generation, branch lifecycle, DB pins, PIDX dependencies, and replacement SHARD coverage
Reclaimer FDB delete activity revalidates branch lifecycle, manifest generation, DB pins, expected PIDX values, and replacement coverage in the delete transaction
DELTA, COMMITS, and VTX rows are deleted only with conditional semantics when stale execution could remove changed data
`clear_range` is not used unless the same transaction fences every key in the range
Hot and cold watermarks are treated as scheduling summaries only and are not sufficient for deletion
Tests cover successful reclaim, stale PIDX rejection, stale manifest rejection, and pinned-version retention
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Resolve namespace-derived pins before deletion
userStories.description: As a namespace fork, I need cheap fork records to protect database history before any DB manager deletes it.
acceptanceCriteria: Namespace fork writes unresolved `NS_FORK_PIN` and `NS_CHILD` facts without eagerly writing one DB pin per inherited database
`NSCAT_BY_DB` records explicit database membership with catalog and tombstone versionstamp windows
`NS_PROOF_EPOCH/{root_namespace_branch_id}` increments on namespace fork, catalog membership, and catalog tombstone mutations
Before install or reclaim planning can delete history, the manager checks relevant unresolved namespace fork facts for the database branch
If a namespace fork can inherit the database at versionstamp V, the manager resolves V through `BR/{branch}/VTX` and `COMMITS` to an exact txid and materializes `DB_PIN(kind=NamespaceFork)`
If the bounded proof is too large, missing, or ambiguous, the manager retains history and logs structured fields
Tests cover lazy namespace-derived pin materialization before DELTA reclaim and retain-history behavior for ambiguous proof
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Implement cold upload and manager publish
userStories.description: As the cold compacter workflow, I need to upload bulky shard payloads to S3 and let the manager publish FDB cold refs.
acceptanceCriteria: Cold compacter accepts `RunColdJob`, validates branch lifecycle and base manifest generation, and uploads one cold shard object per bounded activity
S3 object keys match `db/{database_branch_id_hex}/shard/{shard_id_be_hex:8}/{as_of_txid_be_hex:16}-{object_generation_id}-{content_hash_hex}.ltx`
`object_generation_id` is the cold job id and the same job retry reuses the same object key
Cold compacter reports bounded output refs, sizes, and content hashes through `ColdJobFinished`
Manager cold publish transaction reads branch lifecycle, `CMP/root`, DB pins, namespace proof state, retired-object key for the candidate, and exact covered commit-owned inputs
Manager writes `CMP/cold_shard` refs, advances cold watermarks, and bumps manifest generation
Tests cover idempotent upload, stale cold publish rejection, and read fallback locating a published cold ref
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add retired cold-object grace deletion
userStories.description: As the reclaimer workflow, I need to delete S3 cold objects safely even though FDB snapshots are MVCC and S3 is not.
acceptanceCriteria: Cold object deletion is split into FDB retire, grace-window wait, S3 delete, and FDB cleanup
FDB retire transaction removes the live `ColdShardRef`, writes `CMP/retired_cold_object`, and bumps manifest generation
S3 delete activity runs only after `delete_after_ms` and deletes the exact manager-provided object key
Before calling S3, the activity marks or treats the retired record as `DeleteIssued` for reconciliation
FDB cleanup clears or completes the retired record only if the exact cold ref is still absent and the retired record still matches
The same object key is never republished after retirement, even if a later job produces the same bytes
Tests cover grace-window blocking, exact-key delete, no parallel FDB/S3 delete, and live-ref-to-DeleteIssued corruption logging
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add branch destruction workflow lifecycle
userStories.description: As a branch owner, I need database branch deletion to stop workflow scheduling and complete companion workflows cleanly.
acceptanceCriteria: Extend the database branch record with a monotonic lifecycle generation if it does not already exist
Commits require live writable branch state
Hot install, cold publish, and reclaim transactions read `BRANCHES/list/{database_branch_id}` and abort if missing, deleted, or lifecycle generation changed
Manager handles `DestroyDatabaseBranch` by recording stop state, stopping new scheduling, and signaling all companions to destroy the same branch generation
Companion workflows abandon or finish bounded idempotent work and complete after `DestroyDatabaseBranch`
A manager recreated by stable tags observes FDB branch deletion state and does not schedule new compaction work
Tests cover destroy during idle, destroy during active hot job, and stale generation publish rejection
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add repair logging and orphan cleanup paths
userStories.description: As an operator, I need structured inconsistency handling for stale workflow outputs, orphan staged FDB data, and orphan S3 objects.
acceptanceCriteria: Manager treats unmatched job completions as stale orphan output and schedules cleanup instead of publishing them
Reclaimer can clear staged FDB output that is not referenced by manifest or active workflow state
Reclaimer can delete S3 objects that are referenced by neither live FDB refs, retired-object records, nor active workflow state
FDB live ref to missing S3 object logs corruption and does not silently remove the ref
FDB live ref to a `DeleteIssued` object logs corruption and does not assume the delete is stale
All inconsistency logs include database branch id, manifest generation, object/key ref, job id when available, and selected repair action
Tests cover stale job completion, staged FDB orphan cleanup, S3 orphan cleanup, and corruption logging
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Remove legacy compactor coordination mechanisms
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need the workflow system to be the only compaction writer because this code has not shipped and legacy coexistence is out of scope.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove or disable old standalone hot compactor mutation paths after workflow hot publish is working
Remove or disable old standalone cold compactor mutation paths after workflow cold publish is working
Remove old eviction/global deletion mutation paths as correctness authorities
Remove compactor leases, cold `in_flight_uuid`, pending markers, pending-pin handoff state, and old obsolete/delete queue state from active code paths
Remove `last_hot_pass_txid` as an eviction OCC fence from active deletion logic
No legacy/workflow coexistence mode or rollback compatibility path remains
Regression tests prove commits still go direct to FDB and workflow manager owns publish/delete decisions
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add end-to-end workflow compaction coverage
userStories.description: As a release owner, I need integrated tests that exercise the new architecture across commits, forks, compaction, reclaim, cold storage, and branch deletion.
acceptanceCriteria: Add an end-to-end test that commits multiple deltas, wakes the manager, publishes hot SHARD coverage, reclaims safe DELTAs, and verifies reads
Add a fork race test where a fork or bookmark pin appears after hot job planning and before install, and DELTA deletion is blocked until exact pinned coverage exists
Add a namespace fork test where a lazy namespace-derived DB pin is materialized before history deletion
Add a cold compaction test that publishes cold refs, retires an obsolete cold object, waits the grace window, deletes the exact S3 key, and verifies reconciliation state
Add a branch deletion test that stops all four workflows and rejects stale publishes after lifecycle generation changes
`cargo check -p depot` passes
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
